page: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The instance provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The AMD EPYC processor achieves a strong rating in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25%. However, due to the limited core count, multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the weak category (bottom 25%). Memory bandwidth tests indicate some constraints, with small-block cached reads scoring in the bottom 10% and uncached large-block reads and writes rated as weak. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression perform at average levels.

Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. While database operations and Redis SET operations scored in the poor category (bottom 10%) during testing, the instance remains a candidate for small-scale caching, lightweight memory-bound applications, and basic text processing. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6a.large offers a low-entry point to dedicated AMD EPYC memory-optimized resources, though users requiring high computational throughput or local NVMe storage will face tradeoffs.
description: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, running on an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.6 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a dedicated CPU allocation with 1 physical core and 2 threads. It does not include local storage or GPUs, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show a strong single-core CPU rating (top 25%), but multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not demand high parallel processing power, such as small-scale caching, text processing, and lightweight database tasks. It offers a cost-efficient entry point to dedicated AMD EPYC memory-optimized resources, though it presents tradeoffs in multi-threaded execution and storage speed.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor, suitable for memory-bound workloads requiring moderate compute.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for moderate, memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz
Provides 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps
Suited for small-scale caching and lightweight memory-bound applications
